column80: I think learning materials should include more real-life situations or examples. For me, it is easier to understand a lesson when I can relate it to everyday experiences. When lessons show how concepts are used in daily life, it becomes clearer why the topic is important and how it can be applied outside the classroom. This also makes learning more interesting and meaningful.

column2: Sumasang-ayon ako na lumahok sa pag-aaral na ito. (I agree to participate in this research study)
column5: Working learner / adult learner
column79: Lessons that adjust based on learning pace
column80: In my opinion, students should be given the option to choose how they want to learn. Some students understand lessons better through videos, while others prefer reading or doing interactive activities. If adaptive learning systems allow students to select the format that suits their learning style, it could help them understand the lessons more effectively and stay more engaged.

column80: As an ALS learner, I suggest that learning materials should be simpler and easier to follow. Some learners may find long explanations difficult to understand, so lessons should be broken into smaller parts with clear examples and simple instructions. This can help learners understand the topic better and avoid feeling overwhelmed while studying.

column80: Based on my experience as a student, it would be helpful if learning materials are simple, clear, and easy to follow. Some learners may struggle with long or complicated explanations, so breaking lessons into smaller sections with clear examples can make them easier to understand. This can help students stay motivated and better understand the lessons.
